events.label: reasoning
events.text:
The scenario describes insufficient anchor bolt projection on a heavy-industrial site, work is rough-complete, reference is API RP 686.

API RP 686 (API Recommended Practice 686) covers "Recommended Practices for Machinery Installation and Installation Design". It addresses anchor bolt projection requirements - anchor bolts must have sufficient projection above the baseplate/nut to allow full thread engagement of the nut, typically requiring at least one thread or specific projection above the top of the nut.

Insufficient anchor bolt projection means the anchor bolts don't extend far enough above the baseplate to allow proper nut engagement with full thread contact. This is non-compliant.

Decision: fail
Risk: high (anchor bolts are critical for equipment stability)
Component conditions: installed-defective, non-compliant
Percent complete: rough-complete, maybe 85%
Confidence: high, 0.9
